Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER ALANNA ZABEL'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD

Appearances via Zoom:
Alanna Zabielski (310.913.1315, alanna@aziam.com) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona;
Kajsa M. Minor of Shartsis Friese LLP (415.421.6500, kminor@sflaw.com) for defendant and opposing party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Alanna Zabel's first amended petition to vacate the arbitration award is denied. Zabel has not shown any of the limited grounds in the Federal Arbitration Act allowing for the vacatur of an arbitration award.

Zabel's contention that the award should be vacated because the arbitrator failed to consider her evidence lacks merit. Courts distinguish between an arbitrator's consideration of evidence and the weight assigned to it. It is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award because the arbitrator declined to consider evidence he deemed legally irrelevant, even where that relevance determination is legally incorrect. (Schlessinger v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Susman (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1096, 1110-1111).

Zabel's due process argument fares no better. An arbitrator is not required to conduct evidentiary hearings or afford witness testimony simply because a party requests it.
Zabel's challenges to the arbitrator's procedural rulings are similarly unavailing. An arbitrator's authority to permit motions and manage hearing procedures falls squarely within the broad discretion afforded to the arbitrator. The arbitrator's decisions to postpone the hearing and deny the stay were within that authority.

In short, Zabel has not established that any of the three grounds asserted in her first amended petition warrants vacating the arbitration award. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
